sentence_reviews.text: k-nearest-neighbor methods predict from nearby stored examples, while Naive Bayes applies Bayes’ rule under class-conditional feature assumptions.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA, Ch. 19 nonparametric models including nearest-neighbor models and probabilistic learning; PML nearest neighbors and naive Bayes / Bayesian classifiers topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition is standard and supported across the cited textbook topics, though it combines two methods in one sentence.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Datasets, sampling & splits, Embeddings & distributed representations.
